article_title: Cilium 1.19 Adds Ztunnel Encryption and Strict WireGuard Mode as eBPF Service Mesh Reaches Its Ten-Year Milestone

content_preview.title: Cilium 1.19 Adds Ztunnel Encryption and Strict WireGuard Mode as eBPF Service Mesh Reaches Its Ten-Year Milestone
content_preview.summary: Cilium 1.19 introduces beta ztunnel integration for transparent mTLS encryption without sidecars, strict encryption modes for WireGuard and IPsec, and stable Multi Pool IPAM, as the CNCF-graduated eBPF networking project marks a decade of development.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Cilium 1.19, the latest release of the eBPF-based networking and security platform for Kubernetes, introduces transparent encryption through a new ztunnel integration, mandatory encryption modes for inter-node traffic, and a promotion of Multi Pool IPAM to stable status. The release, which drew [2,934 commits from over 1,010 developers](https://github.com/cilium/cilium/releases/tag/v1.19.0), arrives as the project marks ten years since its first commit.

Cilium is the second-largest CNCF project...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes:
Thorough technical article covering Cilium 1.19 with accurate detail on ztunnel integration, strict encryption modes, and ecosystem context. Summary matches content. GitHub and docs.cilium.io are legitimate primary sources for an open-source project release article. Word count (777) appropriate for News.

source_verification:
- infoq.com: CONFIRMED. Corroborates ztunnel integration, strict IPsec/WireGuard modes, 60%+ deployment share, 75% with managed services, Hubble improvements including drop event tagging, Multi Pool IPAM to stable.
- github.com (release notes): CONFIRMED. Confirms 2934 commits from 1010+ developers, Multi Pool IPAM stable, mutual authentication disabled by default, ICMPv4 destination unreachable for denied egress, deprecated Kafka match fields and ToRequires/FromRequires, Gateway API v1.4.0.
- docs.cilium.io (service mesh): CONFIRMED. Documents Cilium's service mesh capabilities including L4/L7 support and eBPF datapath. Does not specifically mention ztunnel on this page but references mutual authentication beta.
- docs.cilium.io (ztunnel encryption): CONFIRMED. Confirms per-node proxy, transparent L4 mTLS, HBONE protocol on port 15008, namespace enrollment via Kubernetes labels, TCP-only in beta, no Cluster Mesh support, SPIFFE-based identities.
